title: koeleria pubescens subsp. villosa
intro: Koeleria pubescens subsp. villosa, also known as the hairy June grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can provide relief from congestion and help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, offering health benefits for people with respiratory problems. Koeleria pubescens subsp. villosa can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and it is also used in tincture and powder form.
